questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following statements concerning \( \mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{g}) \) is INCORRECT?

options.label: B
options.text: It can be used to make dry ice.

options.label: C
options.text: It can be produced by adding marble to water.

options.label: D
options.text: It generally has a higher percentage in the air in urban areas than that in rural areas.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
How many neutrons and electrons are there in a \( \frac{51}{33}X^{3+} \)  ion?

Number of neutrons    |    Number of electrons

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following substances is an electrolyte?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following is an INCORRECT procedure in titration?

options.label: A
options.text: Rinse the pipette with the solution to be delivered before titration.

options.label: B
options.text: Rinse the conical flask with the solution to be held before titration.

options.label: C
options.text: Take the burette readings with eyes on the same level as the meniscus.

options.label: D
options.text: Make sure that there are no air bubbles in the burette filled with the tritant.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Element X is one of the first twenty elements in the Periodic Table. X forms a stable  \( XH_{4}^{+} \) (aq) ion. Which group of the Periodic Table does X most likely belong to?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Copper(II) phosphate is insoluble in water. What is the number of moles of  \( Cu^{2+}(aq) \)  ions remaining in the solution of the resulting mixture when 0.04 mol of  \( CuCl_{2}(aq) \)  is mixed with 0.02 mol of  \( Na_{3}PO_{4}(aq) \) ?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: A white solid does NOT dissolve in both water and excess aqueous ammonia. Which of the following may this solid be?

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 5: Fossil Fuels and Carbon Compounds
questions.question_text:
The structure of a portion of a polymer is shown below:
[IMG:q8_stem_0.jpg]

Which of the following statements concerning the polymer is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: It can be used as a substitute for glass.

options.label: C
options.text: It can be made from its monomer through addition polymerisation.

options.label: D
options.text: It can decolourise bromine dissolved in an organic solvent quickly.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the following three compounds:
X: \(CH_{3}CH_{2}CH_{2}CH_{2}OH\)    |    Y: \(HOCH_{2}CH_{2}OH\)    |     Z: \(CH_{3}COOCH_{3}\)

Which of the following shows the decreasing order of their solubilities in water?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
6.54 g of zinc granules are added to 100.0 \(cm^{3}\) of 1.0 M \(AgNO_{3}(aq)\). After the reaction has completed, which of the following statements is correct?

(Relative atomic masses: Zn = 65.4, Ag = 107.9)

options.label: A
options.text: Some zinc granules have reacted and no silver ions remain in the solution.

options.label: B
options.text: All the zinc granules have reacted and no silver ions remain in the solution.

options.label: C
options.text: All the zinc granules have reacted and some silver ions remain in the solution.

options.label: D
options.text: The mass of the zinc granules reacted is equal to the mass of the solid product formed.

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
In the electrolysis of 1.0 M  \( CuSO_{4}(aq) \) , copper cathode and carbon anode are used. Which of the following combinations is correct?

Cathode    |    Anode

options.label: B
options.text: Copper dissolves    |    Sulphur dioxide is formed

options.label: C
options.text: Copper is deposited    |    Oxygen is formed

options.label: D
options.text: Copper is deposited    |    Sulphur dioxide is formed

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the following chemical equation :
\( xNH_{3}(g)+yO_{2}(g)\rightarrow xNO(g)+zH_{2}O(g) \)

Which of the following combinations is correct?

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
Consider the following chemical cell :
[IMG:q13_stem_0.jpg]

Which of the following statements is correct?

options.label: B
options.text: The solution in beaker (1) gradually turns brown.

options.label: C
options.text: The solution in beaker (2) gradually changes from pale green to yellow.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
The enthalpy changes of formation of some substances under certain conditions are shown below :
Substance    |    Enthalpy change of formation / kJ mol \( ^{-1} \)
\( H_{2}O(l) \)    |    -286
\( Na_{2}O(s) \)    |    -414
\( NaOH(s) \)    |    -425

What is the enthalpy change of the following reaction under the same conditions?
\( Na_{2}O(s) + H_{2}O(l) \rightarrow 2NaOH(s) \)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: P, Q and R are three different metals. When dilute HCl(aq) is added to these metals separately, only Q and R give a colourless gas. When zinc is added to aqueous solutions of their chlorides separately, only the chloride of R shows no observable change. Which of the following shows the increasing order of the reducing power of the metals?

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 5: Fossil Fuels and Carbon Compounds
questions.question_text:
The molecular formula of compound X is  \( C_{4}H_{7}Br \)  and it has one carbon-carbon double bond. It can react with  \( Br_{2} \)  (dissolved in an organic solvent) to give the following organic product :
[IMG:q16_stem_0.jpg]

Which of the following is / are the possible structure(s) of X?

(1)  \( CH_{2}BrCH_{2}CH=CH_{2} \)
(2)  \( H_{2}C=CHCHBrCH_{3} \)
(3)  \( CH_{3}CH=CHCH_{2}Br \)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Refer to the following set-up :
[IMG:q17_stem_0.jpg]

Which of the following processes can be performed by using the above set-up?

(1) obtaining pure water from sea water
(2) obtaining propane from diesel oil
(3) obtaining oxygen from liquefied air

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
Which of the following pairs of substances, when mixed, would release hydrogen gas?

(1) copper and concentrated HCl(aq)
(2) iron and  \( H_{2}SO_{4}(aq) \)
(3) calcium and NaOH(aq)

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 5: Fossil Fuels and Carbon Compounds
questions.question_text:
Consider the following two compounds :
[IMG:q19_stem_0.jpg]  [IMG:q19_stem_1.jpg]

Which of the following statements is / are correct?

(1) They belong to the same homologous series.
(2) They have the same molecular formula.
(3) They are insoluble in water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
A small piece of sodium is added to water containing a few drops of universal indicator. Which of the following statements is / are correct?

(1) Sodium moves quickly on the water surface.
(2) The resulting solution shows a red colour.
(3) This reaction is exothermic.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following statements are correct?

(1) The standard enthalpy change of formation of graphite is zero.
(2) The standard enthalpy change of combustion of carbon monoxide is a negative value.
(3) The standard enthalpy change of formation of carbon monoxide is equal to the standard enthalpy change of combustion of graphite.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Both A and B are monobasic acids. The pH of 0.10 M A(aq) is 1.0 and the pH of 0.10 M B(aq) is 3.0. Which of the following statements are correct?

(1) A is a stronger acid than B.
(2) Some B molecules are present in B(aq).
(3) Complete neutralisation of 25.0 cm³ of 0.10 M A(aq) and complete neutralisation of 25.0 cm³ of 0.10 M B(aq) require the same number of moles of NaOH(aq).

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 5: Fossil Fuels and Carbon Compounds
questions.question_text:
The simplified diagram below shows how different petroleum fractions can be obtained from a fractionating tower.
[IMG:q23_stem_0.jpg]

Which of the following statements are correct?

(1) Fraction S has a darker colour than fraction Q.
(2) Fraction R has a higher viscosity than fraction P.
(3) Fraction Q is more flammable than fraction P.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
1st statement : Ethene and but-1-ene have the same standard enthalpy change of combustion.
2nd statement : Ethene and but-1-ene have the same empirical formula.

options.label: A
options.text: Both statements are true and the 2nd statement is a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: B
options.text: Both statements are true but the 2nd statement is NOT a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: C
options.text: The 1st statement is false but the 2nd statement is true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
A mixture consists of methane and ethane. 50 cm³ of this mixture completely burns in oxygen to form 80 cm³ of carbon dioxide at room conditions. What is the volume of methane in this mixture at room conditions?

(Molar volume of gas at room conditions = 24 dm³)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
50 cm³ of 0.10 M HCl(aq) reacts with excess calcium carbonate powder in an open conical flask giving curve X in the graph below.
[IMG:q26_stem_0.jpg]

Which of the following changes may give curve Y?

options.label: D
options.text: Use the same mass of calcium carbonate granules instead of calcium carbonate powder.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
The structure of an organic compound is shown below :
[IMG:q27_stem_0.jpg]

Which of the following combinations concerning whether cis-trans isomerism and enantiomerism can occur in the compound is correct?

cis-trans isomerism    |    enantiomerism

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
When chemical equilibrium is attained in a certain reversible reaction, only  \( X(g) \)  and  \( Y(g) \)  are present in a closed container of fixed volume. The following graph shows the variation in concentrations of  \( X(g) \)  and  \( Y(g) \)  with time:
[IMG:q28_stem_0.jpg]

Which of the following equations can represent the reversible reaction?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: When it is heated with excess NaOH(aq), followed by the addition of excess HCl(aq), a major organic product Z is formed. Which of the following is Z?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
When 0.40 mol of  \( SO_{2}(g) \)  and 0.60 mol of  \( O_{2}(g) \)  are placed in a 1.0 dm \( ^{3} \)  evacuated flask, the following reaction occurs.
\( 2SO_{2}(g)+O_{2}(g)\rightleftharpoons2SO_{3}(g) \)

When chemical equilibrium is attained at a certain temperature, the flask is found to contain 0.30 mol of  \( SO_{3}(g) \) . What is the equilibrium constant  \( K_{c} \)  for the reaction at this temperature?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the experimental set-up shown below:
[IMG:q31_stem_0.jpg]

Under room conditions, which of the following pairs of reactants can the progress of their reaction be followed by the above set-up?

(1)  \( \mathrm{Zn(OH)_{2}(s)} \)  and  \( \mathrm{HNO_{3}(aq)} \)
(2) Mg(s) and HCl(aq)
(3) KBr(s) and \(Cl_{2}(aq)\)

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 12: Patterns in the Chemical World
questions.question_text:
Consider the following equilibrium system :
\( 2CrO_{4}^{2-}(aq)+2H^{+}(aq)\rightleftharpoons Cr_{2}O_{7}^{2-}(aq)+H_{2}O(l) \)

Which of the following statements can demonstrate that chromium exhibits the characteristic(s) of transition metals?

(1)  \( Cr_{2}O_{7}^{2-} \) (aq) ions are orange in colour.
(2) Adding HCl(aq) would shift the equilibrium position to the right.
(3) The oxidation states of chromium in  \( CrO_{4}^{2-} \)  and  \( Cr_{2}O_{7}^{2-} \)  are the same.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
The structure of aspirin is shown below :
[IMG:q33_stem_0.jpg]

Which of the following statements about aspirin are correct?

(1) It has an ester group.
(2) It can reduce inflammation.
(3) It has a higher solubility in  \( Na_{2}CO_{3}(aq) \)  than in pure water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following compounds can be used as a monomer for condensation polymerisation?

(1)  \( H_{2}C=CHCH_{2}CH_{2}CH=CH_{2} \)
(2) HOOCCH₂CH₂CH₂CH₂COOH
(3) HOCH₂CH₂CH₂CH₂CH₂CH₂OH

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
The structure of a detergent is shown below :
[IMG:q35_stem_0.jpg]

Which of the following statements concerning this detergent are correct?

(1) It is a soapless detergent.
(2) It can act as an emulsifying agent.
(3) It can increase the surface tension of water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
1st statement : At chemical equilibrium, the concentration of reactants must be equal to the concentration of products.
2nd statement : At chemical equilibrium, both forward reaction rate and backward reaction rate are equal to zero.

options.label: A
options.text: Both statements are true and the 2nd statement is a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: B
options.text: Both statements are true but the 2nd statement is NOT a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: C
options.text: The 1st statement is false but the 2nd statement is true.
